headings: h3: Learn more about the Right To Food UK Commission
pages.text_excerpt: About | Support Local Progress — Get Involved Today — Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby 0 Skip to Content Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up About Ian In Parliament I was first elected as MP for Liverpool West Derby on 12 December 2019 and then for a second time on 4 July 2024. In the 2024 General Election I received 25,302 votes, equal to 66.6% of votes cast. I am immensely proud to be the Parliamentary lead on the Hillsborough Law Now Campaign and the Right To Food Campaign - which you can read more about on this website. In the 2019-2024 Parliament I sat on the Housing, Communities & Local Government Select Committee which scrutinised the policy, administration and spending of the Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government. I also sat on the Environment, Food & Rural Affairs Committee which scrutinised the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs. Hillsborough Law Now Campaign I am immensely proud to lead the Hillsborough Law Now Campaign in Parliament. Our inaugural Parliamentary meeting heard from campaigners on Hillsborough, Grenfell, Blood Contamination, Covid-19 Bereavement, Nuclear Test veterans and from fellow Parliamentarians and legal experts. Find out more . MP of the Year 2022 In March 2022 I was named MP of The Year at the Patchwork Foundation MP Awards in Parliament. At a ceremony in Westminster hosted and presented by Patchwork Patron, Mr Speaker, Rt Hon Sir Lindsay Hoyle MP, I was honoured to win Overall MP of The Year, the foundation’s top honour. Fans Supporting Foodbanks In 2015 I co-founded Fans Supporting Foodbanks, a volunteer community initiative by football fans to tackle food poverty in Liverpool, formed by representatives of Everton FC and Liverpool FC supporters’ groups. Food collections take place outside every home game at Anfield and Goodison Park, with fans of Liverpool FC, Everton FC and visiting teams providing as much as 30% of donations to North Liverpool foodbanks. Follow Fans Supporting Foodbanks on social media at Twitter @SFoodbanks and Facebook @FansSupportingFoodbanks . Learn more about the Right To Food UK Commission You can follow Ian Byrne MP on X (formerly Twitter) , Facebook & TikTok & view clips on his YouTube channel : 545 West Derby Road L13 8AA | 0151 222 2345 | ian.byrne.mp@parliament.uk Privacy Policy Made with Squarespace

headings: h3: For people living in Liverpool West Derby
h3: Learn more about the Right To Food UK Commission
pages.text_excerpt: Contact & Advice | Get Support Today - Contact Now — Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby 0 Skip to Content Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Contact & Advice Contacting Ian Me and my team are here to help. Please use the contact details listed to get in touch. Due to strict Parliamentary protocol, I can only take up casework on behalf of Liverpool West Derby constituents. You can check if I am your MP on the Parliament website and by using this link: https://members.parliament.uk/members/commons email: ian.byrne.mp@parliament.uk phone: 0151 222 2345 write to: Ian Byrne, 545 West Derby Road, L13 8AA in person: please contact the office for details of the MP surgeries I hold around the constituency Vauxhall Law Centre My office and The Vauxhall Law Centre are working together to offer an advice service to all people living in the Liverpool West Derby Constituency. Contact Vauxhall Law Centre at: Phone: 0151 360 7777 Email: advice@vauxhalllawcentre.org.uk Vauxhall Law Centre provides advice on all Benefit and Debt issues and will be able to provide you with advice on a wide range of issues such as: Claiming Personal Independence Payments for people who are sick and people with disabilities Universal Credit Claims, Housing Benefit, Debt and a whole range of further advice Useful Information For people living in Liverpool West Derby Special Educational Needs Pathways Pathways to support understanding of the law relating to Special Educational Needs. These pathways deal with problems related to assessing special educational needs and obtaining educational, health and social care provision. These pathways are now maintained by the Law Clinic at the University of Liverpool with the support of students who study the law relating to children with Special Educational Needs as part of their degree. They are intended as a support tool. https://www.sendecisions.org.uk/ General Coronavirus advice and support For the latest advice and guidance on Coronavirus restrictions, testing advice, Track and Trace support payments, help for carers and help for businesses please visit the Liverpool City Council website using the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/communities-and-safety/emergency-planning/coronavirus/ Struggling to make ends meet - including food and fuel If you need money to buy food, sanitary products, essential items for children, essential clothing or cover fuel costs. If you need white goods, furniture or help to set up home after suffering an emergency or crisis. Call the Mayoral Hardship Fund and Liverpool Citizens Support Scheme on free phone 0800 456 1523 (lines are open from 8am to 6pm, Monday to Friday) Find your nearest foodbank using the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/foodbanks Homeless, at risk of homelessness or in rent arrears Liverpool City Council Housing Options provides help and advice is you are homeless or threatened with homelessness. Call free on 0800 731 6844 Their website also provides further information on the following link: https://liverpool.gov.uk/housing/homeless-or-at-risk/ Benefit and Debt issues - including Universal Credit, Housing Benefit, Personal Independence Payments Ian Byrne MP’s Office and The Vauxhall Law Centre are working together to offer an advice service to all people living in the Liverpool West Derby Const

pages.title: Right to Food UK Commission — Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby
pages.meta_description: Driving urgent scrutiny, evidence, political will and action to secure a legal Right to Food in the UK by 2035.
headings: h3: The work of the Commission - what we are doing
h3: The Right to Food Commission will consider:
h3: UK Right to Food Commissioners
h3: Media coverage of the Right To Food UK Commission:
h3: For information about the Right To Food UK Commission’s Call for Evidence and to submit your evidence to the Commission, please click here.
h3: Learn more about the Right To Food UK Commission
pages.text_excerpt: Right to Food UK Commission — Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby 0 Skip to Content Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Right To Food UK Commission. The Right To Food UK Commission will interrogate and report on the scale and scope of food poverty and hunger in the UK today. The Commission is based out of the UK Parliament and takes evidence from around the UK. It has been established jointly by the Right To Food Campaign, which is led in Parliament by Ian Byrne MP for Liverpool West Derby, the University of Westminster, the Bakers, Food and Allied Workers Union (BFAWU) and the Food and Work Network. The Right To Food UK Commission was formally launched in Parliament on 17th November 2025. Photograph credit: Alexandra Sutton The work of the Commission - what we are doing The objective of The Right To Food UK Commission is to shine a light on the unacceptable nature of food insecurity in Britain, map out solutions to endemic food insecurity and apply pressure on the UK Government, devolved administrations and politicians and policy makers in all UK jurisdictions, to tackle and end food insecurity in the UK. Specifically, the commission will produce a roadmap for the implementation of a Right to Food law in the UK, the aim of which is to support a programme of work and actions to end hunger in the UK by 2035, with an interim objective of halving the number of people experiencing food insecurity by 2030. After formally launching in Parliament on 17th November 2025, the Commission held its first evidence session in Liverpool in January 2026 , followed by sessions in Newcastle, Belfast, Glasgow, Cardiff and London. Each session is preceded by a citizen’s assembly so that peoples’ lived experience fully informs the work of the Commission. Photograph credit: Alexandra Sutton Commissioners outlining the work of the Right To Food UK Commission - Parliament, November 2025 How to get involved with the Commission The Right to Food Commission will consider: The extent and nature of food insecurity across the UK, including the various nations and regions and specific localities The effects of malnutrition and food insecurity on public health outcomes in the UK The impact of current labour market conditions, welfare provision, and asylum/immigration policy as drivers of food insecurity The role of public policy frameworks in tackling food insecurity – and the potential for interventions by Central Government, devolved national and regional bodies, directly-elected Mayors and local authorities How to ensure support and investment for collective community-based food initiatives How best to legislate for an effective and enforceable statutory Right to Food. The considerations above will be framed around the five demands of the Right To Food campaign: 1. Universal Free School Meals – Free school meals for all children 2.Transparency on Food Costs in Wages and Benefits – Government must reveal how food costs are used in wage and benefit calculations 3. Independent Enforcement – Create a regulatory body to ensure government accountability 4. Community Kitchens – Fund community dining and meal programmes 5. Ensured Food Security – Integrate food security into all policy areas UK Right to Food Commissioners Chair: Barrie Margetts, Emeritus Professor, University of Southampton Vice Cha

pages.h1: New Right To Food UK Commission launches in Westminster
headings: h3: Learn more about the Right To Food UK Commission
pages.text_excerpt: News | Get Involved Today — Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby 0 Skip to Content Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu Ian Byrne MP - Working for West Derby About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Open Menu Close Menu About Contact & Advice Your Page Moss West Derby Centre for Social Justice Right To Food UK Commission Right To Food Campaign Hillsborough Law Now Campaign 'The Real Truth' Legacy Project Sign Up Ian Byrne 11/17/25 Ian Byrne 11/17/25 New Right To Food UK Commission launches in Westminster Later today I will officially launch the Right to Food UK Commission in Parliament (Monday 17 November). Its central mission is to provide a Right to Food roadmap to end hunger and foodbank Britain. Read More Ian Byrne 7/1/25 Ian Byrne 7/1/25 Speaking out against Universal Credit & PIP Bill at 2nd Reading Today I told the Government that its Universal Credit and PIP Bill is shambolic, because it is. Watch my speech. Read More Ian Byrne 6/20/25 Ian Byrne 6/20/25 Assisted Dying Bill: why I’m voting NO Today I am voting NO to the Assisted Dying Bill, following much research and deliberation. Watch my video to find out more. Read More Ian Byrne 6/12/25 Ian Byrne 6/12/25 Questions for the Government on delay to Hillsborough Law Today I urged the Government to introduce a Hillsborough Law worthy of the name or be seen as part of the ongoing betrayal of survivors and families of Hillsborough and all those affected by state cover ups. Read More Ian Byrne 3/27/25 Ian Byrne 3/27/25 Speaking to Sky News: the impact of welfare cuts on constituents The Government's plans to cuts to support for disabled people will push 250,000 people - including 50,000 children - into poverty, according to the Government's own analysis. Today I spoke to Sky News about why I cannot and will not support these cuts. Read More Ian Byrne 3/17/25 Ian Byrne 3/17/25 Pensions compensation: Speaking up for 50s-born women Today in Parliament I urged the Government to change course and award compensation to all 1950s-born women affected by changes to their pension age. #Waspi #CEDAWInLaw Read More Ian Byrne 2/10/25 Ian Byrne 2/10/25 End toxic and dehumanising narrative on immigration Today in Parliament I called out the toxic, dehumanising political narrative around immigration and the shameless posturing of some politicians on this issue. Read More Ian Byrne 12/18/24 Ian Byrne 12/18/24 Watch: I ask PM to let MPs vote on Waspi compensation Today at PMQs I asked the Prime Minister to let MPs vote on whether the compensation should in fact be paid. Read More Ian Byrne 12/13/24 Ian Byrne 12/13/24 Letter sent to Livv Housing CEO re industrial dispute Today I have written to the Livv Housing CEO urging her to work with Unite and Unison to bring the current industrial dispute to an end. Read More Ian Byrne 12/12/24 Ian Byrne 12/12/24 EDM tabled on Livv Housing dispute Today I have tabled a Parliamentary motion regarding the ongoing industrial dispute at Livv Housing Group. Read More Ian Byrne 11/29/24 Ian Byrne 11/29/24 My petition for proper funding for hospices launched today Whatever your view on assisted dying, everyone agrees end-of-life care needs more funding. That's why I've launched a petition calling for proper funding for hospices. Read More Ian Byrne 11/29/24 Ian Byrne 11/29/24 Today I will be voting against the Terminally Ill Adults (End of Life) Bill Today I will be voting against the Terminally Ill Adults (End of Life) Bill.
